templates.title: BirdNET-Go
templates.description: Real-time bird sound identification using BirdNET. Analyzes audio from a microphone or RTSP stream and identifies bird species.

env.name: TZ
env.label: Timezone
env.description: Container timezone (IANA format, e.g. America/New_York, Europe/Helsinki)

env.name: BIRDNET_UID
env.label: User ID
env.description: Linux user ID that owns config, audio clips, and database files. Must match host user for correct file permissions. Run 'id -u' on host to find yours.

env.name: BIRDNET_GID
env.label: Group ID
env.description: Linux group ID that owns config, audio clips, and database files. Must match host user for correct file permissions. Run 'id -g' on host to find yours.

env.name: WEB_PORT
env.label: Web UI Port
env.description: Host port for the web interface

env.name: BIRDNET_LOCALE
env.label: Locale
env.description: Language for species names (e.g. en-us, fi, de, sv)

env.name: BIRDNET_LATITUDE
env.label: Latitude
env.description: Your location latitude (-90.0 to 90.0) for species range filtering

env.name: BIRDNET_LONGITUDE
env.label: Longitude
env.description: Your location longitude (-180.0 to 180.0) for species range filtering

templates.note:
After deployment, access the web UI at http://your-host:8080.

Configuration

  • User ID / Group ID — Linux UID/GID that will own config files, audio clips, and the database. Must match your host user for correct file permissions. Run id -u and id -g on the host to find your values.
  • Timezone — Affects timestamps in detections and logs. Use IANA format (e.g. Europe/Helsinki, America/New_York).
  • Locale — Language for bird species names (e.g. fi, de, sv, en-us). Leave empty for English.
  • Latitude / Longitude — Your location coordinates for species range filtering. Only species expected in your area will be shown.

Audio Input

For local microphone capture, the host device /dev/snd must be accessible to the container. For RTSP camera streams, configure the stream URL in the web UI after first launch.

Important

Enable "Pull the image" when deploying to ensure you get the latest nightly build. Otherwise Docker may use a cached older version.

See GitHub for full documentation.

templates.title: BirdNET-Go (AutoTLS)
templates.description: BirdNET-Go with automatic HTTPS certificates via Let's Encrypt. Use this for internet-facing deployments with a domain name.

env.name: BIRDNET_HOST
env.label: Domain Name
env.description: Your domain name for TLS certificate (e.g. birds.example.com). Required.

env.name: TZ
env.label: Timezone
env.description: Container timezone (IANA format, e.g. America/New_York, Europe/Helsinki)

env.name: BIRDNET_UID
env.label: User ID
env.description: Linux user ID that owns config, audio clips, and database files. Must match host user for correct file permissions. Run 'id -u' on host to find yours.

env.name: BIRDNET_GID
env.label: Group ID
env.description: Linux group ID that owns config, audio clips, and database files. Must match host user for correct file permissions. Run 'id -g' on host to find yours.

env.name: BIRDNET_LOCALE
env.label: Locale
env.description: Language for species names (e.g. en-us, fi, de, sv)

env.name: BIRDNET_LATITUDE
env.label: Latitude
env.description: Your location latitude (-90.0 to 90.0) for species range filtering

env.name: BIRDNET_LONGITUDE
env.label: Longitude
env.description: Your location longitude (-180.0 to 180.0) for species range filtering

templates.note:
This template enables automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt. Your domain must point to this server and ports 80/443 must be accessible from the internet.

Required: Set your domain name in the BIRDNET_HOST variable.

Configuration

  • User ID / Group ID — Linux UID/GID that will own config files, audio clips, and the database. Must match your host user for correct file permissions. Run id -u and id -g on the host to find your values.
  • Timezone — Affects timestamps in detections and logs. Use IANA format (e.g. Europe/Helsinki, America/New_York).
  • Locale — Language for bird species names (e.g. fi, de, sv, en-us). Leave empty for English.
  • Latitude / Longitude — Your location coordinates for species range filtering. Only species expected in your area will be shown.

Audio Input

For local microphone capture, the host device /dev/snd must be accessible to the container. For RTSP camera streams, configure the stream URL in the web UI after first launch.

Important

Enable "Pull the image" when deploying to ensure you get the latest nightly build. Otherwise Docker may use a cached older version.

See GitHub for full documentation.
